---
package: "openpipeline"
version: "v4.2.0"
title: "from_tiledb_to_h5mu"
namespace: "Convert"
description: ""
type: "module"
toc: false
latest: "true"
subtitle: "openpipeline v4.2.0"
---

::: {.column-margin}

### Info

ID: `from_tiledb_to_h5mu`  
Namespace: `convert`

### Links

[{{< fa brands github >}} View source code](https://github.com/openpipelines-bio/openpipeline/tree/v4.2.0/src/convert/from_tiledb_to_h5mu)

[![](/images/viash-hub-logo.svg){.vh-icon} Browse all versions](https://www.viash-hub.com/packages/openpipeline)

[![](/images/viash-hub-logo.svg){.vh-icon} Get run instructions](https://www.viash-hub.com/packages/openpipeline/v4.2.0/components/convert/from_tiledb_to_h5mu)

:::




## Argument groups

### Input database

Open a tileDB-SOMA database by URI.


|Name                   |Description                                                                           |Attributes                                        |
|:----------|:--------------------------------------------------|:--------------------|
|`--input_uri`          |A URI pointing to a TileDB-SOMA database.                                             |`string`, required, example: `"s3://bucket/path"` |
|`--s3_region`          |Region where the TileDB-SOMA database is hosted.                                      |`string`                                          |
|`--endpoint`           |Custom endpoint to use to connect to S3                                               |`string`                                          |
|`--s3_no_sign_request` |Do not sign S3 requests. Credentials will not be loaded if this argument is provided. |`boolean`, default: `FALSE`                       |
|`--input_modality`     |TileDB-SOMA measurement to take the input from.                                       |`string`, required                                |
|`--input_layers`       |Layers to import from TileDB-SOMA database.                                           |List of `string`, required, multiple_sep: `";"`   |


### MuData output

|Name                   |Description                                                                                                    |Attributes                                 |
|:----------|:--------------------------------------------------|:--------------------|
|`--output`             |The output MuData file.                                                                                        |`file`, required, example: `"output.h5mu"` |
|`--output_modality`    |Name of the modality to write the results to.                                                                  |`string`, required                         |
|`--output_compression` |Compression format to use for the output AnnData and/or Mudata H5 files. By default no compression is applied. |`string`, example: `"gzip"`                |

## Authors


  * Dries Schaumont [{{< fa brands github >}}](https://github.com/DriesSchaumont) [{{< fa brands orcid >}}](https://orcid.org/0000-0002-4389-0440) [{{< fa brands linkedin >}}](https://linkedin.com/in/dries-schaumont) (author, maintainer)

## Example commands

You can run the pipeline using `nextflow run`.

### View help

You can use `--help` as a parameter to get an overview of the possible parameters.

```bash
nextflow run openpipelines-bio/openpipeline \
  -r v4.2.0 -latest \
  -main-script target/nextflow/convert/from_tiledb_to_h5mu/main.nf \
  --help
```

### Run command

<details>
<summary>Example of `params.yaml`</summary>

Generate a `params.yaml` for this component on [Viash Hub](https://www.viash-hub.com/packages/openpipeline/v4.2.0/components/convert/from_tiledb_to_h5mu).

</details>

Run locally with:

```bash
nextflow run openpipelines-bio/openpipeline \
  -r v4.2.0 -latest \
  -profile docker \
  -main-script target/nextflow/convert/from_tiledb_to_h5mu/main.nf \
  -params-file params.yaml
```

:::{.callout-note}
Replace `-profile docker` with `-profile podman` or `-profile singularity` depending on the desired backend.
:::

Running on Seqera or through Viash Hub is explained in the [guides](/guides/index.qmd).
